content.full_text:
CommissioIl, 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance; Administration 
Executive/Legal 
Enforcement 
Conflict of Interest 
(916) 322·5660 
Vigo G. Nielsen, Jr. 
Dobbs & Nielsen 
One Maritime Plaza, Suite 2500 
The Alcoa Building 
San Francisco, CA 
94111 
Dear Mr. Nielsen: 
322·5901 
322-6441 
December 27, 1977 
Re: 
Opinion Request 
No. 77-044 
Thank you for your letter of November 21, 1977, 
requesting an opinion with respect to campaign disclosure by 
Visitacion Associates and its parent companies. 
Because 
your question does not raise a substantial question of in-
terpretation of the Political Reform Act, no formal opinion 
will be issued. 
However, I hope the following informal 
advice will answer your questions. 
322·6444 
You stated that Visitacion Associates is a general 
business partnership that is jointly owned by AMFAC Communities, 
Inc.-San Francisco and Crocker Land Company. 
AMFAC Communities, 
Inc.-San Francisco is a wholly owned subsidiary of AMFAC, 
Inc. 
Crocker Land Company is a wholly owned subsidiary of 
Foremost-McKesson, Inc. 
Visitacion Associates has made and 
expects to make campaign contributions. 
AMFAC, AMFAC Communities, 
Inc.-San Francisco, Foremost-McKesson, Inc. and Crocker Land 
Co. have made or may in the future make campaign contributions. 
Based on these facts you asked whether contributions made by 
Visitacion Associates will be attributed to the parent corporations. 
As you are aware, in the opinion requested by 
Harry H. Kahn, representing American Building Maintenance 
Industries, 2 FPPC Opinions 151 (No. 75-185, Nov. 3, 1976), 
we held that a corporation and its wholly owned subsidiaries 
are Ha combination of persons" within the meaning of Government 
Code Section 82013 unless it is clear from surrounding circumstances 
that the corporation and its wholly owned subsidiaries act 
completely independently. 
Applying the standard set forth 


Vigo G. Nielsen, 
December 27, 1977 
Page Two 
in Kahn to the instant situation, AMFAC, Inc. and its wholly 
owned subsidiary AMFAC Communities, Inc.-San Francisco should 
aggregate their contributions unless the two companies act 
completely independently when making contributions. 
Similarly, 
Foremost-McKesson, Inc. and Crocker Land Company should file 
joint statements. 
However, the Kahn opinion is not dispositive 
when contributions are made by a-cO:mpany such as Visitacion 
Associates which is jointly owned by two other corporate 
entities. 
If Visitacion is managed and controlled independently 
of the parent companies, we do not believe that contributions 
made by the partnership are attributable to either parent. 
Consequently, Visitacion should file separate reports disclosing. 
only its own contributions. 
If Visitacion is jointly controlled 
by both parents, the parent corporations and Visitacion 
should file joint reports with respect to the contributions 
made by Visitacion. 
The parent corporations should report 
other contributions separately. 
Finally, if Visitacion's 
contributions are controlled by one of the two parents, 
Visitacion should file jointly with that parent. 
Although it is difficult to formulate standards 
that will be appropriate in every case, we believe that the 
general guidelines set forth above will result in full disclosure 
and minimize confusion, benefiting both the company and the 
public. 
If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion 
request, you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18321. 
If you have any questions, please contact Natalie 
West, an attorney with the Commission's Legal Division.

sections.question: Whether contributions made by Visitacion Associates will be attributed to the parent corporations (AMFAC, Inc., AMFAC Communities, Inc.-San Francisco, Foremost-McKesson, Inc., and Crocker Land Company), given that Visitacion Associates is a general business partnership jointly owned by AMFAC Communities, Inc.-San Francisco and Crocker Land Company.
sections.conclusion: If Visitacion is managed and controlled independently of the parent companies, contributions made by the partnership are not attributable to either parent, and Visitacion should file separate reports. If Visitacion is jointly controlled by both parents, the parent corporations and Visitacion should file joint reports with respect to Visitacion's contributions, with parent corporations reporting other contributions separately. If Visitacion's contributions are controlled by one parent, Visitacion should file jointly with that parent. For wholly owned subsidiaries like AMFAC Communities and Crocker Land Company, they should aggregate contributions with their parents unless acting completely independently.
sections.question_synthetic: Should campaign contributions made by Visitacion Associates, a partnership jointly owned by two corporate entities, be attributed to or aggregated with contributions from its parent companies for disclosure purposes under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Attribution of campaign contributions from a jointly-owned partnership depends on the degree of independent management and control: independent management means separate reporting; joint control means joint reporting; and control by one parent means filing jointly with that parent. For wholly-owned subsidiaries, aggregation with parent companies is required unless complete independence is demonstrated.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal opinion issued; informal advice letter instead. Document clearly distinguishes between wholly-owned subsidiaries (which should aggregate with parents) and jointly-owned partnerships (attribution depends on independent control). References prior opinion Kahn (75-185).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether contributions made by Visitacion Associates will be attributed to the parent corporations (AMFAC, Inc., AMFAC Communities, Inc.-San Francisco, Foremost-McKesson, Inc., and Crocker Land Company), given that Visitacion Associates is a general business partnership jointly owned by AMFAC Communities, Inc.-San Francisco and Crocker Land Company.

CONCLUSION: If Visitacion is managed and controlled independently of the parent companies, contributions made by the partnership are not attributable to either parent, and Visitacion should file separate reports. If Visitacion is jointly controlled by both parents, the parent corporations and Visitacion should file joint reports with respect to Visitacion's contributions, with parent corporations reporting other contributions separately. If Visitacion's contributions are controlled by one parent, Visitacion should file jointly with that parent. For wholly owned subsidiaries like AMFAC Communities and Crocker Land Company, they should aggregate contributions with their parents unless acting completely independently.

Similarly, Foremost-McKesson, Inc. and Crocker Land Company should file joint statements. embedding.summary: FPPC provides informal advice on campaign disclosure obligations for Visitacion Associates, a partnership jointly owned by subsidiaries of AMFAC, Inc. and Foremost-McKesson, Inc., determining that contribution attribution depends on whether the partnership is independently managed or controlled by one or both parents.
